items.title: Per2 deficiency in microglia alleviates motor dysfunction by inhibiting ferroptosis in spinal cord injury
items.author: Hechun Xia
items.content: Circadian rhythms gene Per2 regulates microglial ferroptosis through the PPARα-GPX4 axis in SCI, which indicates that Per2 may be emerging as a potential therapeutic target for SCI treatment.

items.title: Cerebrospinal fluid-contacting neurons are sensory neurons with uniform morphological and region-specific electrophysiological properties in the mouse spinal cord
items.author: Nicolas Wanaverbecq
items.content: Morphological and electrophysiological understanding of cerebrospinal fluid-contacting neurons (CSF-cNs) is extended by analysis in the mouse, revealing that they express PKD2L1 and ASICs along with ligand- and voltage-gated channels modulated by metabotropic receptors.

items.title: Nitrogen addition alters arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and soil bacteria networks without promoting phosphorus mineralization in a semiarid grassland
items.author: Cheng Gao
items.content: A study on steppe grasslands found that nitrogen addition alters arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and bacterial networks but does not promote phosphorus mineralization.

items.title: STRaM: A genetic framework for improved cell product provenance for research and clinical translations
items.author: Wei Guo
items.content: STRaM is an advanced bioinformatic pipeline for handling complex engineered-cell and cell-line provenance. Our automated system evaluates similarity index, purity index, editing/mutation index for cell products identification and traceability.

items.title: Information dynamics and the emergence of high-order individuality in ecosystems
items.author: Henrik J. Jensen
items.content: Quantifying information-theoretic individuality as persistence of information in time reveals the emergence of higher-order organization in evolutionary systems, where groups of species exhibit enhanced collective persistence.

items.title: Predictive capabilities of polygenic scores in an East-Asian population-based cohort: the Singapore Chinese health study
items.author: Rajkumar Dorajoo
items.content: The authors analyzed over 2000 polygenic scores (PGSs) to evaluate their associations with 58 baseline traits in the Singapore Chinese Health Study (SCHS) cohort.

items.title: Targeting VCAM-1 with chimeric antigen receptor and regulatory T cell for abdominal aortic aneurysm treatment
items.author: Jian Zhang
items.content: This study investigates CAR-Treg cells targeting VCAM-1 as a novel treatment for abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA). Preclinical results show CAR-Treg cells effectively reduce inflammation and aortic dilation, offering a non-surgical treatment option for AAA.

items.title: FANTASIA leverages language models to decode the functional dark proteome across the animal tree of life
items.author: Rosa Fernández
items.content: FANTASIA, a protein language model-based tool, enables large-scale functional annotation across ~1000 animal proteomes, revealing novel gene functions in both model and non-model organisms beyond homology-based methods.
